channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Argentina's Martinez praises referees' performance at FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Argentina defender Lisandro Martinez has dismissed controversies surrounding refereeing decisions during the FIFA World Cup 2026, commending match officials for their performance after Egypt criticize...

item.title: Quebec's Population Shifts: Regional Growth and Decline Projections
item.description: New projections from the Institut de la statistique du Québec (ISQ) reveal that Quebec's population growth will become increasingly uneven across the province. While regions like the Capitale-National...

item.title: Dutch Migrants' Legacy and Integration in Post-WWII Australia
item.description: The article explores the experiences of Dutch migrants who settled in Australia after World War Two, focusing on their cultural preservation, language shift, and historical trauma. David Van Berkel's ...

item.title: A Voyage Through Love and Loss: Navigating Relationships on the Open Sea
item.description: This reflective memoir recounts a tumultuous relationship aboard a boat journey to Hawaii, where the author grapples with emotional insecurity, trust issues, and conflicting beliefs. The narrative unf...

item.title: FCC Approves Solar Reflecting Satellite Despite Astronomical Concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has approved Reflect Orbital's Earendil-1 test satellite, a 60-by-60-foot mirror designed to reflect sunlight back to Earth after dark. This technology aims...

item.title: Tanzania's CBRM Project Aims to Maintain 15,000km of Roads Year-Round
item.description: The Tanzania Rural and Urban Roads Agency (TARURA) has initiated a three-year Community-Based Routine Maintenance (CBRM) project, funded by the World Bank, to ensure over 15,000 kilometers of roads re...

item.title: Edo LG Elections Peaceful as Governor Okpebholo Votes
item.description: The Edo State Governor, Monday Okpebholo, cast his vote during the Local Government elections, describing the process as peaceful. He praised voters for their orderly conduct and noted reports from ac...

item.title: Social Media Manager Harriet Njenga Resigns from Peter Salasya's Team
item.description: Harriet Njenga, a social media manager for Mumias East MP Peter Salasya, has resigned from her role and shared her resignation letter publicly on Instagram. Her departure followed an interview where S...

item.title: EU Demands Meta Modify Addictive Features on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: The European Commission has charged Meta Platforms' Instagram and Facebook with breaching tech rules by designing features that keep users hooked. Regulators are demanding changes to autoplay and infi...

item.title: Balochistan's administrative restructuring divides Quetta into two districts
item.description: The Balochistan government has announced significant administrative changes, including splitting Quetta district into East and West Quetta along the railway line. This restructuring increases the prov...

item.title: Australian Turf Expert Oversees World Cup Pitch Maintenance in Los Angeles
item.description: Justin Lang, a turf manager from Melbourne, has been stationed in California since May to oversee the preparation and maintenance of Los Angeles Stadium for the FIFA World Cup. His role involves ensur...

item.title: Deputy Minister Letsike Faces Ethical Scrutiny Over SAPC Involvement
item.description: Deputy Minister Mmapaseka Steve Letsike is under scrutiny over her continued membership on the South African Pharmacy Council (SAPC) following ethical concerns raised by Health Minister Aaron Motsoale...

item.title: Daniel Tupou achieves hat-trick in milestone 300th NRL game against Parramatta Eels
item.description: Daniel Tupou celebrated a historic milestone in his NRL career by scoring a hat-trick in his 300th game, a rare achievement that highlights his longevity and skill. The match, against the Parramatta E...

item.title: Jobs Resilient to AI: Future Career Opportunities in Key Sectors
item.description: The article explores which jobs are less vulnerable to AI disruption across various industries. In healthcare, roles like pharmacists, doctors, and nurses remain critical due to their clinical decisio...

item.title: Pula Focuses on Tanzanian Legal Battle After Abandoning South African Appeal
item.description: Pula Graphite has decided to abandon its appeal against a South African high court ruling that exonerated Patrice Motsepe from a R3.4bn liability, stating it is not a retreat but a strategic move to f...

item.title: Government to Boost Energy Sector Investment with Reforms
item.description: The Tanzanian government has committed to creating a favorable investment environment for the energy sector, aiming to attract both local and international investors. This initiative is crucial as the...

item.title: Security Lapses and Culture of Corruption Raise Fears at Port Coquitlam Jail
item.description: The North Fraser Pretrial Centre (NFPC) in Port Coquitlam, B.C., faces severe security concerns and a toxic work environment. Staff and former employees report systemic issues including lax hiring pra...

item.title: Traffic Police Educate Students on Road Safety in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Traffic police in Dar es Salaam are intensifying road safety education programs in schools to reduce accidents involving children. Officer Bumu Mwalugenge emphasized that educating pupils on traffic l...

item.title: Pakistan's Counter-Terrorism Operations in Balochistan Result in Significant Terrorist Killings
item.description: State media reported that 23 terrorists were killed in ongoing operations in Balochistan, raising the total casualty count since July 5 to 102. The operations, part of Operation Shaban, targeted group...

item.title: Moses Simon's Struggles with Rangers: Ethnicity and Football Challenges
item.description: Super Eagles winger Moses Simon has spoken out about his challenging experiences with Nigerian Premier Football League clubs, particularly Rangers International, during his early career. The 30-year-o...
